docs.title: Introduction to Information Technology
chunks: Information technology covers hardware, software, computer networks, and data. Its goal is to process, store, and transmit information efficiently.
The information processing cycle includes data collection, processing, storage, and distribution. Information systems support decision making in organizations.
Information security relies on the CIA triad: Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability. Access control and regular backups are core practices.
Cloud computing provides on-demand resources via three service models: IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS. The cloud enables fast scaling but requires cost governance.
The history of the Internet began with ARPANET in 1969 as a project of the United States Department of Defense. TCP/IP became the standard protocol suite in the 1980s.
Object-oriented programming organizes software design around data, or objects, rather than functions and logic. Key principles include encapsulation, inheritance, and polymorphism.
Sorting algorithms arrange items in order. Bubble sort runs in O(n^2) time, while merge sort and quicksort run on average in O(n log n).
Graphic design uses visual hierarchy, typography, and color theory to communicate ideas. It is distinct from coding and is unrelated to computer security policies.
The Krebs cycle is a series of biochemical reactions used by aerobic organisms to release stored energy through the oxidation of acetyl-CoA into ATP and carbon dioxide.

topics.prompt: Generate questions about the CIA triad and basic security practices.
Generate questions about cloud service models and their trade-offs.

docs.title: Relational Database Fundamentals
chunks: A relational database management system organizes data into tables of rows and columns. The primary key uniquely identifies each record.
Normalization reduces redundancy and inconsistencies. 1NF requires atomic values, 2NF removes partial dependencies, and 3NF removes transitive dependencies.
Indexes speed up read queries but slow down writes due to the cost of maintaining the index. The trade-off depends on workload.
Transactions follow ACID properties: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, and Durability, ensuring correctness under failures or concurrent access.
MapReduce is a programming model for processing large data sets with a parallel, distributed algorithm on a cluster. It is unrelated to relational normalization.
Recurrent neural networks process sequential data by maintaining hidden state across time steps. They are used in natural language processing and speech recognition.
HTTP is a stateless application layer protocol used by the World Wide Web. Cookies and tokens are typical mechanisms to add session state on top of HTTP.
Photosynthesis converts light energy into chemical energy stored in glucose. It occurs in the chloroplasts of plant cells.
The Renaissance was a period of cultural rebirth in Europe between the 14th and 17th centuries, marked by renewed interest in classical art and humanism.

topics.prompt: Generate questions applying ACID properties to banking transactions.

docs.title: Operating Systems Principles
chunks: An operating system manages processes, memory, I/O devices, and the file system. It provides the interface between users and hardware.
Common CPU scheduling algorithms include FCFS, SJF, and Round Robin. Round Robin suits time-sharing systems thanks to its quantum.
Paging divides memory into frames and pages to avoid external fragmentation. A page fault occurs when a page is not present in RAM.
Deadlock occurs when processes wait for each other's resources. The four Coffman conditions are mutual exclusion, hold and wait, no preemption, and circular wait.
REST is an architectural style for distributed systems that uses HTTP verbs to operate on resources. It is unrelated to CPU scheduling decisions inside a kernel.
Functional programming treats computation as the evaluation of mathematical functions and avoids changing state and mutable data. Haskell and Elixir are examples.
Plate tectonics describes the large-scale motion of seven major and many minor plates of the Earth's lithosphere, causing earthquakes and forming mountains.
Keynesian economics is a macroeconomic theory of total spending in the economy and its effects on output, employment, and inflation, developed by John Maynard Keynes.
A vector space is a set of objects called vectors which can be added together and multiplied by scalars. It is a foundational concept in linear algebra.

topics.prompt: Generate questions comparing FCFS, SJF, and Round Robin.
Generate questions about deadlock and the four Coffman conditions.

exams.name: IT Fundamentals Midterm
sample_questions: In the CIA triad, which property ensures that data cannot be modified without authorization?
Which scenario fits the SaaS service model best?

exams.name: Computer Science Core Final
sample_questions: Compare the advantages and disadvantages of indexes in a relational database management system.
Why is Round Robin generally fairer than FCFS in time-sharing systems?
